seo.home_desc: IGNITE is highly optimized software for automated detection of local inflammation sites in thermal images. Developed for Jugend forscht 2026.
seo.home_keys: IGNITE, Jugend forscht, thermography, inflammation detection, thermal image, hotspot, diabetic foot syndrome, image processing, Jona Noack
seo.algo_title: The Algorithm – 5 Image Processing Pipeline Stages
seo.algo_desc: Detailed explanation of the mathematical pipeline of IGNITE: kernel scaling, body segmentation, top-hat transform, statistical thresholding, and geometric noise filtering.
seo.algo_keys: IGNITE, image processing, Otsu binarization, top-hat, Chamfer, Union-Find, algorithm
seo.arch_desc: The hybrid computation stack of IGNITE: PyTorch CUDA GPU (<10ms), Rust CPU with Rayon (~30ms), and Python fallback. Lemire Monotone Deque O(N) and Chamfer integer optimizations.
seo.arch_keys: Rust, PyO3, Rayon, CUDA, PyTorch, Monotone Deque, Lemire, performance, optimization

problem.title: Early Detection Saves Limbs
problem.description: Diabetic Foot Syndrome (DFS) is one of the most common complications of diabetes mellitus. Undetected inflammation sites on the feet can lead to severe tissue damage – in the worst case, amputation. IGNITE uses thermal imaging cameras and advanced image processing to detect local inflammation early and automatically.

features.title: Two Specialized Analysis Modes
features.feature1_desc: Automatically detects and marks thermal hotspots across the entire body – joints, back, hands. Each heat island is isolated, numbered, and measured in detail.
features.feature2_desc: Pairwise zone comparison of both feet. The system anatomically divides into forefoot, midfoot, and heel, comparing temperature distribution to detect asymmetric heat patterns.
features.feature3_desc: Automatic selection of the fastest available backend: NVIDIA GPU (< 10 ms), Rust CPU (~30 ms), or Python fallback (~80 ms). Maximum speed on any hardware.

cta.title: Ready to try IGNITE?
cta.subtitle: Download the free desktop application and analyze your own thermal images.

algorithm.title: The Algorithm
algorithm.subtitle: A five-stage mathematical image processing pipeline for precise extraction of pathological inflammation sites.
algorithm.step1_desc: To process images independently of resolution, the system scales the radii of all morphological operators proportionally to the image width. The kernel is made bitwise odd and limited to at least 3×3 pixels.
algorithm.step2_desc: The object is separated from the background using Otsu binarization. The threshold is constrained to a physiological temperature interval. A Euclidean distance transform (Chamfer-3-4 metric) and adaptive erosion eliminate edge artifacts.
algorithm.step3_desc: Isolates local brightness peaks by subtracting the morphological opening from the original image. Compensates for global temperature gradients and highlights local heat sources.
algorithm.step4_desc: Calculates mean μ and standard deviation σ of the top-hat intensity across all body pixels. A pixel is classified as an inflammation site upon significant deviation. An absolute heat filter eliminates false positives in cold areas.
algorithm.step5_desc: Connected-component analysis using Union-Find. Pixel groups are discarded if too small or of implausible shape. Circularity filtering removes line-shaped noise.

architecture.title: System Architecture
architecture.subtitle: A hybrid computation architecture for maximum performance on any hardware.
architecture.backend_desc: At startup, the most performant backend is automatically selected:
architecture.gpu_desc: Processes data directly in GPU memory (VRAM). Perfect for batch processing large datasets.
architecture.rust_desc: Native Rust binary with parallelized computations across all CPU cores via Rayon.
architecture.python_desc: Ensures executability on any system without CUDA or Rust compiler.
architecture.opt1_desc: 1D erosion/dilation with Lemire algorithm. 10-50× speedup for large kernels.
architecture.opt2_desc: Chamfer metrics with u32 integers instead of f64 floats. Single scaling at the end.
architecture.opt3_desc: Normalization via ndarray::Zip distributed across all CPU cores.
architecture.opt4_desc: OrderedDict with 20 entries prevents uncontrolled RAM growth.
architecture.opt5_desc: 350 ms debounce for sliders. No unfinished background threads during fast adjustments.

about.title: About the Project
about.subtitle: IGNITE is a Jugend forscht project by Jona Noack from Pforzheim, Germany.
about.dev_bio: I'm Jona, 15 years old, a student from Pforzheim, Germany. IGNITE is my Jugend forscht 2026 project in the field of Mathematics/Computer Science. The idea came from the desire to use modern image processing for a medical purpose – the early detection of inflammations that are often recognized too late.
about.project_desc: IGNITE (Thermographic Inflammation Detection) is a desktop application for automated detection of local inflammation sites in thermal images. The software combines mathematical image processing with a hybrid computation architecture of Python, Rust, and CUDA for maximum performance.
about.motivation_desc: Diabetic Foot Syndrome affects millions of people. Through early detection of asymmetric temperature patterns on the feet, inflammations can be identified before they cause irreversible damage. My goal is to make this technology accessible and easy to use.
about.contact_desc: Questions, feedback, or interested in collaboration?

footer.disclaimer: Not a substitute for medical diagnosis
